gold.rationale: 公开可核对的信息仅能支持“按通报完成约7小时出舱并安装空间碎片防护装置、人员分工与返回舱段”等事实；文本将这些事实进一步解读为“疑似碎片击中后的临时修补、险情与隐瞒”，属于把不确定猜测包装成主要叙事结论。配图中两张关键“疑点图”经裁剪、对比度/锐化与红圈箭头标注后被当作证据使用，但其所指向的“裂痕/碎屑/擦伤”无法从原始公开通报中得到验证，且与呈现“作业顺利、状态稳定”的画面在语义与证据指向上不匹配，整体误导性大于真实成分，因此判为 Mostly False。

atoms: A close-up view of an astronaut's gloved hand interacting with a complex piece of space station hardware.
The astronaut is wearing a white spacesuit with a textured black grip on the glove.
The background shows dark space and parts of the station structure, including a grid-like surface.
The image has a slightly grainy, low-light quality typical of spacewalk footage.
An astronaut in a white spacesuit is being assisted by a robotic arm on the space station.
A red circle highlights a small, dark spot on a metallic panel of the station.
A zoomed-in inset box shows the same spot with red arrows pointing to it.
Chinese text annotations label the spot as '疑似擦痕/碎屑 (网友圈出)' (Suspected scratch/debris (highlighted by netizens)).
A watermark in the bottom left reads '舱外航天服图像b' (EVA spacesuit image b).
A watermark in the bottom right reads '@广西日报' (Guangxi Daily).
A logo in the top right corner reads '天链' (Tianlian).
A split-screen image showing a screenshot of a video feed.
The top banner displays the text '03-16 19:xx 截图' (Screenshot 03-16 19:xx).
The left panel is labeled '原画面' (Original frame) and shows an astronaut's arm and part of the station.
The right panel shows a cropped, zoomed-in section of the astronaut's arm with red outlines highlighting the suit and a screen.
A watermark '@人民日报' is visible in the bottom left of the left panel.
An illustration of the Earth surrounded by a dense field of white dots representing space debris.
The Earth is depicted with blue oceans and white clouds.
The debris forms a ring-like structure around the planet, suggesting an orbital path.
The background is black space.
An astronaut in a white spacesuit is being moved by a robotic arm in space.
The astronaut is positioned above a large, curved module of the space station.
The scene is brightly lit, likely by station lights, against the black background of space.
A logo in the bottom right corner reads '新华社 XINHUA NEWS'.
An astronaut in a white spacesuit is floating in space with the Earth visible in the background.
The astronaut is interacting with a robotic arm attached to the space station.
The Earth shows blue oceans and white cloud formations.
The image is split horizontally, with the top part showing a different view of the station structure.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A close-up shot of an astronaut's gloved hand working on a piece of space station equipment. The image is dark and grainy, focusing on the interaction between the hand and the machinery.
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows a generic spacewalk scene without specific text overlays, aligning with the post's description of 'standard' footage used in discussions.

visual_indicators.scene_description: An image of an astronaut and a robotic arm on the space station, heavily annotated with red circles, arrows, and Chinese text. The annotations point to a specific spot on the station's surface, labeling it as a 'suspected scratch or debris'.

visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image directly visualizes the post's claim about 'suspected scratches/debris' by using overlays to highlight a specific area on the station, matching the narrative of 'netizen-circulated' evidence.

visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows a screenshot with a specific timestamp, which aligns with the post's discussion of 'screenshots' being circulated to support claims about the mission timeline.

visual_indicators.scene_description: An illustration depicting the Earth surrounded by a ring of white dots representing space debris. The image is stylized and symbolic.
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: This image is a generic illustration of space debris, which aligns with the post's theme of 'space debris' being a potential cause for the mission's unusual nature.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A bright, high-contrast photo of an astronaut being moved by a robotic arm on the space station. The scene is set against the blackness of space.
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: This is a standard press photo from Xinhua News, showing a routine-looking spacewalk, which contrasts with the 'suspicious' narrative discussed in the post.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A split-screen image showing an astronaut floating in space with the Earth in the background. The astronaut is interacting with a robotic arm.
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: This image shows a standard spacewalk scene with the Earth in the background, which aligns with the post's description of 'smooth-looking' footage that is then interpreted as evidence of a crisis.

retrieved_evidence.text: 有关神舟二十一号乘组出舱活动的讨论中，有描述提到空间站舱体外壁、设备边缘等位置存在细微擦痕或疑似碎屑痕迹。根据航天工程人员介绍，长期在轨飞行的空间站外表面不可避免会受到微流星体、空间环境和频繁舱外作业的综合影响，因此在设备舱段和外壁结构上出现局部磨损或沉积物属于工程设计考虑范围内的情况。相关技术团队会通过在轨巡检、地面数据分析和后续维护安排，对这些细节进行系统评估与记录。
【神舟二十一号航天员乘组圆满完成第二次出舱活动】据中国载人航天工程办公室消息，北京时间3月16日19时35分，经过7小时的出舱活动，神舟二十一号乘组航天员张陆、武飞、张洪章密切协同，在空间站机械臂和地面科研人员的配合支持下，圆满完成空间碎片防护装置安装等任务。出舱航天员张陆、武飞已安全返 ​​​

【#神21乘组圆满完成第二次出舱活动#】3月16日19时35分，经过7小时的出舱活动，神舟二十一号乘组航天员张陆、武飞、张洪章密切协同，在空间站机械臂和地面科研人员的配合支持下，圆满完成空间碎片防护装置安装等任务。出舱航天员张陆、武飞已安全返回问天实验舱，出舱活动取得圆满成功。

至此，张陆已 ​​​
【#神21乘组完成第二次出舱#】#航天员张陆已出舱活动6次# ​​​​据中国载人航天工程办公室消息，北京时间2026年3月16日19时35分，经过7小时的出舱活动，神舟二十一号乘组航天员张陆、武飞、张洪章密切协同，在空间站机械臂和地面科研人员的配合支持下，圆满完成空间碎片防护装置安装等任务。出舱航天 ​​​
多家媒体近日从中国载人航天工程办公室获悉，神舟二十一号乘组在第二次出舱活动中，重点执行了空间碎片防护装置安装等多项在轨任务。参与任务的技术专家介绍，此类装置旨在提升空间站对微小碎片的防护能力，出舱航天员需在轨完成支架固定、组件连接和外观检查等工序。除防护装置相关操作外，任务还包括对部分舱外设施的例行巡检和状态记录。
中国空间站配备的大型机械臂是支持神舟二十一号等任务开展舱外作业的关键设备之一。公开资料显示，该机械臂具备多自由度关节，可在核心舱与实验舱之间转运货物、搬运大型舱外设备，并辅助航天员在轨作业和位姿调整。根据航天工程部门的介绍，机械臂在任务过程中会根据预定程序和实时指令进行频繁姿态调整，以适应不同航程和负载需求，同时还需配合空间站整体姿态控制系统，保持组合体结构安全和动力学稳定。
多家媒体本周集中转发中国载人航天工程办公室发布的消息称，神舟二十一号乘组第二次出舱活动在空间站轨道段顺利实施。通报以简要方式介绍了出舱时间、参加人员、主要作业内容及任务完成情况，强调天地协同配合和地面科研团队的技术支持作用。多家报道援引有关人士观点认为，本次舱外作业进一步检验了空间站相关系统的在轨工作能力，对后续长期运行具有示范意义。
多家媒体近日援引中国载人航天工程办公室消息报道，正在执行长期在轨飞行任务的神舟二十一号乘组本周展开了本次飞行阶段的第二次出舱活动。相关报道指出，此次出舱是围绕空间站在轨维护与升级安排实施的一项既定任务，旨在在轨检验多项设备性能，并为后续航天活动积累经验。有关方面称，此次任务在既定轨道条件下组织实施，体现了我国空间站运营管理的常态化水平。
据多家媒体转发的中国载人航天工程办公室通报，历时约7小时的神舟二十一号乘组第二次出舱活动圆满完成，期间既定任务目标基本实现。出舱航天员张陆、武飞已安全返回问天实验舱，完成舱门关合、密封性检查及舱内环境恢复等后续程序。据介绍，地面科研和工程团队正在对任务过程中采集的图像数据和设备运行参数进行整理分析，为后续在轨工作提供参考。
据多家媒体转引中国载人航天工程办公室通报，神舟二十一号乘组第二次出舱活动按计划于北京时间2026年3月16日19时35分开始实施。报道援引任务一线人员介绍称，在地面系统综合评估空间站姿态、轨道环境及各项设备状态后，指令于当晚下达，乘组成员随即按程序完成舱外作业相关准备。相关细节仍在进一步梳理当中。
据多家媒体援引中国载人航天工程办公室介绍，在神舟二十一号乘组第二次出舱活动中，执行舱外作业的为航天员张陆、武飞，两人通过舱门依次出舱，利用空间站机械臂和自身移动能力开展多点位操作。舱内航天员张洪章则负责配合出舱流程，与地面科研人员保持稳定通信支持，对舱体状态和仪表参数进行监视。报道指出，天地协同在关键步骤中多次进行口令核对和状态确认。
在对神舟二十一号出舱过程的技术分析中，一些讨论聚焦于同一舱段局部区域在不同视角和放大比例下呈现的细节差异，试图解读设备表面的划痕、遮挡物或阴影变化。航天领域专家指出，空间站外壁和设备表面通常覆盖有多层防护材料、光学传感器和电缆束，受光照角度、反射条件及航天员作业姿态影响，局部纹理和亮暗变化较为复杂，需要结合在轨工程数据、材料特性和标准工况，才能对所谓前后变化或异常作出科学判断。
多家媒体根据中国载人航天工程办公室发布的信息报道，神舟二十一号乘组本次出舱活动总时长约7小时。任务期间，出舱航天员围绕舱外工作区域逐项执行既定操作，包括设备转运、装调以及状态复核等环节，地面控制中心通过测控系统对关键节点进行实时监测和调度。据参与任务的技术人员介绍，此次出舱作业节奏总体平稳，各项技术指标基本符合预期。
空间碎片风险一直是中国空间站和神舟二十一号乘组在轨安全评估中的重要因素之一。根据科普资料，地球低轨道区域分布着大量报废卫星、火箭残骸及微小碎片，飞行速度高、碰撞能量大，可能对在轨航天器构成威胁。因此，空间站设计采用了多层防护板、被动防护装置以及必要时的规避机动方案，同时在舱外作业任务规划中预留对防护装置进行检查、加装或加固的步骤，以提升整体抗碎片能力，降低轨道碎片撞击风险。
神舟二十一号任务所在的中国空间站长期在轨运行，需要复杂的电源、热控和通信系统保障。太阳翼阵列在各舱段展开，为组合体提供主要电力来源，其方位角和俯仰角会随轨道位置与太阳高度自动调整。站内外布设的大量天线和数据链路维系与地面测控站的实时通信，使航天员在执行舱外作业、设备维护和科学实验过程中能够随时接受指令、回传状态信息，并在必要时得到地面专家的技术支持。
在空间站运营阶段，中国航天科技集团和航天员科研训练中心等机构承担神舟二十一号等任务的总体技术管理与飞行控制工作。根据近年来公开的任务新闻，这些机构在每次出舱活动前需进行多轮地面模拟演练，包括对外部结构、关键设备及潜在风险源的梳理，明确航天员行走路径和作业顺序。任务执行过程中，地面指挥团队会依据遥测数据和航天员口头反馈，适时对作业步骤进行优化，确保舱外活动与空间站关键设备运行互不干扰。
神舟二十一号乘组在轨期间还承担了多项科学实验平台的集成与调试工作，包括材料科学、生物医药和空间物理等领域。根据相关研究团队的公开介绍，这些实验装置多数安装在空间站实验舱内，但部分需要通过舱外安装平台与舱外暴露载荷接口对接，以直接暴露于太空环境下运行。航天员在舱外作业中需要完成机械连接、电气插拔和状态确认等操作，确保实验装置在轨稳定工作并按计划采集科学数据。
围绕神舟二十一号和中国空间站的社会讨论中，公众关注点不仅集中在单次出舱活动，还包括长期在轨运营对关键设备可靠性的考验。工程技术人员指出，空间站系统采用模块化冗余设计，对推进、供电、环境控制与生命保障等关键环节设置多套备份，在局部组件出现异常时可通过切换通路和在轨维修方式维持整体功能。同时，地面团队会定期对历史任务数据进行复盘，总结舱外作业经验，以提升后续任务的风险识别与管理能力。
神舟二十一号任务被官方描述为圆满完成，中国空间站组合体继续在轨稳定运行。根据公开资料，执行该任务的乘组在轨期间按计划进行了多次舱外作业，航天员身着新一代舱外航天服，通过气闸舱进出核心舱外，完成设备安装、检测和维护等工作。相关报道多以正规新闻宣传风格呈现航天员在轨作业的标准场景，强调任务组织周密、技术状态可靠以及中国载人航天工程体系化管理的成熟度。

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reports the core facts of the EVA (time, duration, personnel, task) matching the evidence, but it significantly distorts the narrative by framing the routine task as a potential emergency repair and inventing specific unverified details about scratches and communication issues.
text_analysis.t1_evidence: The post claims 'internal group chat screenshots' and 'private aerospace circle messages' allege 'short-term danger' and 'astronauts had minor scratches', whereas the evidence (src_366_1, src_366_2, src_366_7) confirms the mission was a planned routine task with no mention of danger, scratches, or unofficial sources.
t1_problematic_subclaims: Claims 'internal group chat screenshots' and 'private aerospace circle messages' exist regarding the mission.
Asserts astronauts suffered 'minor scratches' or 'surface wear' during the EVA.
States there was a 'short-term danger' or 'communication link fluctuation' forcing an early conclusion.
Suggests the 'space debris protection device installation' was actually a 'temporary patch' for suspected damage.
text_analysis.t2_evidence: The post frames the 'installation of space debris protection devices' as a 'temporary patch' for 'suspected damage' and implies a 'rushed conclusion' due to 'danger', whereas the evidence (src_366_4, src_366_6, src_366_10) describes it as a 'planned routine task' and 'routine inspection' with 'stable' operation.
t2_problematic_subclaims: Characterizes the routine 'installation of space debris protection devices' as an 'emergency repair' or 'temporary patch' for damage.
Implies the mission was 'rushed' or 'forced to end early' due to 'short-term danger'.
Portrays the 'safe return' as a sign of 'nervous moments' rather than standard procedure.
Interprets standard 'tool package opening/closing' as evidence of 'on-site processing' of damage.
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is factually grounded in the official timeline and personnel but relies heavily on speculative 'internal' narratives to distort a routine maintenance mission into a dramatic emergency scenario.

images.scene_description: A close-up shot of an astronaut's gloved hand interacting with complex machinery on the exterior of a space station, likely during an EVA. The lighting is harsh and directional, typical of space photography.
images.explanation: A clean, unedited news photo of an astronaut working on the space station exterior.

images.scene_description: A cropped section of a space station EVA showing a robotic arm and an astronaut. The image has been heavily edited with red circles, arrows, and zoomed-in insets to highlight specific surface details.

images.v2_evidence: The image contains multiple red circles, arrows, and inset zooms overlaid on the original photo to draw attention to specific surface features, which is a clear visual editing cue.
images.explanation: A news photo that has been visually edited with red annotations and zoomed-in insets to highlight specific details, consistent with the post's description of 'suspected scratch/debris' evidence.

images.v2_evidence: The image is a composite showing a 'screenshot' alongside a version with red outlines tracing the astronaut's body and hand, indicating digital manipulation and editing.
images.explanation: A composite image presenting a broadcast screenshot alongside a digitally traced version, used to highlight specific details as described in the post.

images.scene_description: A diagrammatic illustration showing the Earth surrounded by a dense field of white dots representing space debris or satellites in orbit.
images.v1_evidence: The image is a stylized, computer-generated illustration rather than a photograph, depicting a conceptual view of space debris around Earth, which matches the post's discussion of space fragment risks.
images.explanation: A synthetic, illustrative image depicting space debris, used to visually represent the topic of space fragment risks discussed in the post.

images.scene_description: A wide shot of an astronaut being moved by a robotic arm on the exterior of the space station. The astronaut is illuminated brightly against the dark background of space.
images.explanation: A clean, official news photo of an astronaut being moved by a robotic arm during an EVA.

images.scene_description: A wide shot of an astronaut floating outside the space station with Earth visible in the background. The astronaut is interacting with the station's structure.
images.explanation: A clean, unedited news photo of an astronaut working on the exterior of the space station with Earth in the background.

image_analysis.global_notes: The post uses a mix of clean news photos and heavily edited/annotated images to support its narrative about 'suspected anomalies' in the EVA. The edited images (V2) are used to highlight specific details that the post claims are evidence of a rushed or emergency operation.

judge.level3_rationale: 该帖子捏造了所谓的‘内部群聊截图’和‘私下消息’来支持其关于任务紧急和航天员受伤的虚假叙事，同时使用了合成插图和经过编辑的图片来强化误导，导致核心信息完全失实。
judge.reasoning_trace: The post fabricates specific claims about 'internal group chats' and 'private messages' (T1) and distorts the nature of the mission from routine to emergency (T2). The post includes a synthetic illustration of space debris (V1) and heavily edited images with overlays to support its narrative (V2). With T1, T2, V1, and V2 all firing, the post lacks a surviving true core and meets the criteria for 'False'.
judge.core_claim: The official press release for Shenzhou-21's second EVA hides a 'temporary patch' mission due to suspected damage, short-term danger, and unreported astronaut injuries.
key_subclaims: The official press release implies a 'rushed conclusion' and 'short-term danger' due to a 'temporary patch' mission.
There are 'internal group chat screenshots' and 'private aerospace circle messages' alleging 'short-term danger' and 'astronauts had minor scratches'.
The images show 'suspected scratches/debris' and 'cropped edge details' that prove the mission was an emergency repair.
The official release's phrase 'safely returned' implies a tense moment or incident occurred.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The official press release implies a 'rushed conclusion' and 'short-term danger' due to a 'temporary patch' mission.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence states the operation was 'smooth' and 'rhythm was generally stable', contradicting the claim of a rushed conclusion due to danger.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: There are 'internal group chat screenshots' and 'private aerospace circle messages' alleging 'short-term danger' and 'astronauts had minor scratches'.
subclaim_findings.note: No evidence supports the existence of these specific internal messages; they are presented as rumors without corroboration.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The images show 'suspected scratches/debris' and 'cropped edge details' that prove the mission was an emergency repair.
subclaim_findings.note: While images show netizen annotations of 'suspected scratches', evidence clarifies these are normal wear and tear, not proof of an emergency repair.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The official release's phrase 'safely returned' implies a tense moment or incident occurred.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence describes the mission as 'smooth' and 'routine', making the implication of a tense incident unsupported by the actual operational context.

verdict_reasoning: The post fabricates specific claims about 'internal group chats' and 'private messages' (T1) and distorts the nature of the mission from routine to emergency (T2).
The post includes a synthetic illustration of space debris (V1) and heavily edited images with overlays to support its narrative (V2).
With T1, T2, V1, and V2 all firing, the post lacks a surviving true core and meets the criteria for 'False'.
The anti-bias rule 'T1+V1 with companions => False' applies here.

image_findings.explanation: Standard EVA footage showing astronaut interaction with hardware, consistent with evidence.
Image contains heavy overlays (red circles, arrows, text) highlighting 'suspected scratches', matching the post's narrative of netizen-circulated evidence.
Composite image showing a screenshot with red outlines, matching the post's description of 'cropped edge details' used to support claims.
Image is a stylized, computer-generated illustration of space debris, not a photograph, matching the 'illustration' style flagged by the analyzer.
Standard press photo from Xinhua News showing a routine-looking spacewalk, consistent with evidence.
Standard news broadcast still showing an astronaut with Earth in the background, consistent with evidence.
